utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format specifies five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five of these items. Each one is fully specified: health_coverage includes provider, coverage description, monthly cost, and benefits; property_coverage includes provider, coverage description, monthly cost, and benefits; travel_coverage includes provider, coverage description, monthly cost, and benefits; additional_services includes provider, coverage description, monthly cost, and benefits; total_budget includes monthly_total, annual_total, and activation_date. All details are provided according to the required format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four LifeFirst policies: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, and additional_services. To map them to ratings, I look for LifeFirst options in the ground-truth data. For health_coverage, the matching entry is LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 8) since it mentions physical and mental health with telemedicine and wellness programs. For property_coverage, the matching entry is LifeFirst Standard Coverage (rating 8) under home_content. For travel_coverage, the corresponding entry is LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 7) under travel_insurance. For additional_services, the coverage description matches Premium Insurance Membership (rating 10) from general_services, as it provides concierge and advisor benefits. These are the best matches by provider and description.
